title: Fix Login Page Display and Internet Identity Verification Flow

requirements.summary: Restore LoginPage.tsx to a fully functional, visually complete login page with AstraVault military branding and automatic redirect for authenticated users
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to `/login` renders the AstraVault login page with matte black background and gold/green accents
The 'Login with Internet Identity' button is visible and triggers the Internet Identity login popup when clicked
An already-authenticated user visiting `/login` is automatically redirected to `/`
The page uses Rajdhani/Inter fonts consistent with the military theme
No blank screen, missing content, or JS errors appear on the login route

file_operations.description: Restore the login page to render a fully styled AstraVault-branded login interface with matte black (#0a0a0a) background, gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) and military-green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) accents, shield/command iconography (use the generated shield logo at frontend/public/assets/generated/astravault-shield-logo.dim_256x256.png), and Rajdhani/Inter fonts. Render a single 'Login with Internet Identity' button that calls the `login` function from `useInternetIdentity`. Add a useEffect that checks if the user is already authenticated (identity resolved and non-anonymous) and redirects to `/` using React Router's navigate. Show a loading spinner while `loginStatus === 'logging-in'`. Ensure the page is accessible at the `/login` route for all unauthenticated users.

requirements.summary: Configure InternetIdentityProvider with correct environment-specific identityProvider URL to prevent Internet Identity iframe hanging
acceptanceCriteria: In development mode (`import.meta.env.DEV === true`), `identityProvider` resolves to `http://rdmx6-jaaaa-aaaaa-aaadq-cai.localhost:4943`
In production mode, `identityProvider` resolves to `https://identity.ic0.app`
The Internet Identity popup opens successfully without hanging on 'Securing Connection'
No hardcoded or unreachable identity provider URL exists in `main.tsx`

file_operations.description: Update the `InternetIdentityProvider` configuration to set the `identityProvider` prop dynamically based on the environment. Use a ternary expression: if `import.meta.env.DEV` is `true`, set `identityProvider` to `http://rdmx6-jaaaa-aaaaa-aaadq-cai.localhost:4943`; otherwise, set it to `https://identity.ic0.app`. Remove any hardcoded, unreachable, or malformed identity provider URL that causes the Internet Identity iframe to hang on 'Securing Connection'. Ensure the provider wraps the App component correctly.

requirements.summary: Restore useActorReady hook to strictly validate all three readiness conditions before returning true
acceptanceCriteria: `isActorReady` is `false` while `isInitializing` is `true`
`isActorReady` is `false` when the principal equals `Principal.anonymous()`
`isActorReady` is `false` when the actor instance is `null`
`isActorReady` flips to `true` only after all three conditions are simultaneously satisfied
No premature `true` value causes queries to fire before the actor is ready

file_operations.description: Restore the hook logic so that `isActorReady` is computed as `true` only when all three conditions are strictly met in order: (a) `isInitializing` from `useInternetIdentity` is `false`, (b) the identity is non-null and the principal is non-anonymous (not equal to `Principal.anonymous()`), and (c) the actor instance returned by `useActor` is non-null. Use a useMemo or direct boolean computation to ensure the hook never flips `isActorReady` to `true` prematurely. Export `isActorReady` as a stable boolean.

requirements.summary: Restore useQueries.ts to gate all queries on isActorReady and handle null profile responses correctly
acceptanceCriteria: `useGetProfile` does not fire before `isActorReady` is `true`
The React Query cache key includes the principal string (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`)
A `null` response from `getProfile` sets `data` to `null` and does not set `isError` to `true`
A thrown exception from `getProfile` sets `isError` to `true`
All other backend-calling queries in `useQueries.ts` are gated on `isActorReady`

file_operations.description: Restore the `useGetProfile` query hook so its `enabled` flag is strictly gated on `isActorReady === true`. Update the React Query cache key to include the authenticated principal string (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`) so it auto-invalidates when the principal changes. Ensure the query treats a `null` or empty optional returned by the backend as 'profile not found' (new user flow), not as an error — only a thrown exception should result in `isError: true`. Audit all other query hooks in the file (e.g. custom links, vault notes) and ensure their `enabled` flags also gate on `isActorReady` to prevent premature queries.

requirements.summary: Restore ProtectedRoute.tsx to implement a strict state machine with loading, error, unauthenticated redirect, new user modal, and authenticated outlet rendering
acceptanceCriteria: A full-page military-themed loading spinner is shown while `isActorReady` is `false` or profile query is loading
No redirect to `/login` occurs while loading is in progress
A 'Retry' button with gold accent is shown when the profile query errors; clicking it calls `refetch()`
Unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login` only after actor initialization is confirmed complete
New users (null profile) see `<ProfileSetupModal>` instead of being redirected
Authenticated users with a profile see the protected page content (`<Outlet />`)

file_operations.description: Restore the component to implement a strict state machine: (1) While `isActorReady` is `false` OR the profile query `isLoading` is `true`, render a full-page military-themed loading spinner (with matte black background, gold spinner accent) — do not redirect or render children. (2) If `isError` is `true` (profile query threw an exception), render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (identity is null or anonymous AND actor initialization is complete), redirect to `/login` using React Router's Navigate. (4) If `data` is `null` (profile not found, new user), render `<ProfileSetupModal>` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present, render `<Outlet />` (the protected route children). The component must never redirect to `/login` during the loading phase.

requirements.summary: Restore App.tsx to Version 4 routing structure with AuthGuard rendering only a loading spinner until isInitializing settles
acceptanceCriteria: Routes `/login`, `/`, `/vault`, and `/settings` are all defined and functional
While `isInitializing` is `true`, only the full-page loading spinner is rendered — no routing decision is made
After `isInitializing` settles to `false`, unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login`
Authenticated users can access `/`, `/vault`, and `/settings`
No blank screen or premature redirect occurs during the initialization phase

file_operations.description: Restore the App.tsx routing structure to Version 4 with routes: `/login` (public, renders LoginPage), `/` (protected, renders DashboardPage), `/vault` (protected, renders VaultPage), `/settings` (protected, renders SettingsPage). Ensure the `AuthGuard` (or root layout component wrapping protected routes) checks `isInitializing` from `useInternetIdentity` and renders only the full-page military-themed loading spinner while `isInitializing` is `true`. The component must never redirect to `/login` or render protected content until `isInitializing` has definitively settled to `false`. After initialization completes, unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login`, and authenticated users proceed to the requested protected route.

requirements.summary: Restore ProfileSetupModal.tsx to Version 4 design with client-side SHA-256 hashing and query cache invalidation on success
acceptanceCriteria: New users (null profile) see the ProfileSetupModal after Internet Identity login
The modal collects name, username, optional email, and vault password
The vault password is SHA-256 hashed before being sent to the backend
On successful profile creation, the profile React Query cache is invalidated and the modal closes, revealing the dashboard
The modal is styled with matte black background, gold accents, and military-green buttons consistent with the military theme

file_operations.description: Restore the ProfileSetupModal to the Version 4 design: a full-screen modal (Dialog component from shadcn/ui or custom overlay) styled with matte black (#0a0a0a) background, gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) accents, and military-green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) buttons. The modal collects name, username, optional email, and vault password (with a confirmation field). On form submission, hash the vault password client-side using SHA-256 (via the utility in frontend/src/utils/encryption.ts) before calling the `registerUser` mutation. On successful registration, invalidate the React Query cache key `['profile', principalText]` so `ProtectedRoute` immediately transitions from the modal to the dashboard. Include validation for required fields and password confirmation matching.
